title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat by exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines. This could lead to widespread data encryption, service outages, and potential data exfiltration, severely impacting business operations.
recommended_actions: Immediately review and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing strong authentication, access controls, and regular code integrity checks.
Ensure all cloud infrastructure is backed up regularly and that backups are stored in isolated environments to prevent ransomware encryption.
Deploy advanced threat detection tools to monitor for suspicious activity within cloud environments and CI/CD workflows.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: A vulnerability was identified in code-projects Chamber of Commerce Membership Management System...
description: A vulnerability was identified in code-projects Chamber of Commerce Membership Management System 1.0. Impacted is the function fwrite of the file admin/pageMail.php. The manipulation of the argument mailSubject/mailMessage leads to command injection. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via command injection, potentially compromising sensitive data and system integrity.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and sanitization for mailSubject and mailMessage parameters in admin/pageMail.php.
Update to the latest patched version of the software if available or contact the vendor for guidance.
Monitor systems for unusual activity and implement Web Application Firewall (WAF) rules to block exploitation attempts.

title: A weakness has been identified in Belkin F9K1122 1.00.33. The impacted element is the function...
description: A weakness has been identified in Belkin F9K1122 1.00.33. The impacted element is the function formSetPassword of the file /goform/formSetPassword of the component Parameter Handler. This manipulation of the argument webpage causes stack-based buffer overflow. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5043.

title: A vulnerability was determined in mxml up to 4.0.4. This issue affects the function index_sort of...
description: A vulnerability was determined in mxml up to 4.0.4. This issue affects the function index_sort of the file mxml-index.c of the component mxmlIndexNew. Executing a manipulation of the argument tempr can lead to stack-based buffer overflow. The attack is restricted to local execution. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. This patch is called 6e27354466092a1ac65601e01ce6708710bb9fa5. A patch should be applied to remediate this issue.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in mxml up to version 4.0.4 allows for a stack-based buffer overflow via manipulation of the 'tempr' argument in the index_sort function. This could lead to application crashes or arbitrary code execution if exploited. However, the attack requires local execution, limiting the attack surface.
affected_industries: Healthcare (if mxml is used in medical devices)
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the patch identified as 6e27354466092a1ac65601e01ce6708710bb9fa5 to mitigate the vulnerability.
Audit systems and applications using mxml to identify and update any instances running versions up to 4.0.4.
Implement monitoring and logging to detect unauthorized local access attempts that could exploit this vulnerability.

title: A vulnerability has been found in code-projects Accounting System 1.0. This affects an unknown...
description: A vulnerability has been found in code-projects Accounting System 1.0. This affects an unknown part of the file /view_work.php of the component Parameter Handler. Such manipulation of the argument en_id leads to sql injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows for SQL injection via the 'en_id' parameter in the /view_work.php file, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or database manipulation. Since the exploit is publicly disclosed, the risk of exploitation is significantly increased.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply input validation and sanitization for the 'en_id' parameter in the /view_work.php file to prevent SQL injection.
Update or patch the Accounting System 1.0 software if a fix is available from the vendor or community.
Monitor server logs for unusual activity or exploitation attempts targeting the vulnerable endpoint.

title: A vulnerability was found in D-Link DIR-513 1.10. This issue affects the function formSetEmail of...
description: A vulnerability was found in D-Link DIR-513 1.10. This issue affects the function formSetEmail of the file /goform/formSetEmail. Performing a manipulation of the argument curTime results in stack-based buffer overflow. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used. This vulnerability only affects products that are no longer supported by the maintainer.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the D-Link DIR-513 1.10 firmware allows remote attackers to exploit a stack-based buffer overflow via the curTime parameter in the formSetEmail function. This could lead to unauthorized remote code execution, potentially compromising the device and any connected networks.
recommended_actions: Immediately replace or upgrade affected devices to newer models that are actively supported and receive security updates.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices from critical systems and sensitive data.
Disable remote management features on the affected devices if replacement is not immediately possible.

title: A vulnerability was detected in Totolink A3600R 4.1.2cu.5182_B20201102. Affected by this issue is...
description: A vulnerability was detected in Totolink A3600R 4.1.2cu.5182_B20201102. Affected by this issue is the function setNoticeCfg of the file /cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi of the component Parameter Handler. The manipulation of the argument NoticeUrl results in command injection. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Totolink A3600R allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands via command injection in the setNoticeCfg function. This could lead to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or full system compromise.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply any available firmware updates or patches provided by Totolink to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict access to the affected device by implementing network segmentation and firewall rules to limit exposure to untrusted networks.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and implement intrusion detection/prevention systems to identify potential exploitation attempts.

title: The Twentig plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the ...
description: The Twentig plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the 'featuredImageSizeWidth' parameter in versions up to, and including, 1.9.7 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated users with Contributor-level access or higher to inject malicious scripts into pages via the 'featuredImageSizeWidth' parameter. This could lead to unauthorized script execution, data theft, session hijacking, or defacement of the website, potentially affecting user trust and data integrity.
recommended_actions: Update the Twentig plugin to a patched version as soon as it becomes available.
Restrict Contributor-level access to only trusted users and review user roles and permissions.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious payloads targeting this vulnerability.

title: GRID::Machine versions through 0.127 for Perl allows arbitrary code execution via unsafe...
description:
GRID::Machine versions through 0.127 for Perl allows arbitrary code execution via unsafe deserialization.

GRID::Machine provides Remote Procedure Calls (RPC) over SSH for Perl. The client connects to remote hosts to execute code on them. A compromised or malicious remote host can execute arbitrary code back on the client through unsafe deserialization in the RPC protocol.

read_operation() in lib/GRID/Machine/Message.pm deserialises values from the remote side using eval()

  $arg .= '$VAR1';

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with unknown severity. CVE: CVE-2026-4851.

title: CVE-2026-3427 - The Yoast SEO – Advanced SEO with real-time guidance and built-in AI plugin for WordPress is vulnera
description: The Yoast SEO – Advanced SEO with real-time guidance and built-in AI plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the the `jsonText` block attribute in all versions up to, and including, 27.1.1 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Contributor-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with Contributor-level access or higher to inject malicious scripts into pages via the `jsonText` block attribute. These scripts can execute whenever a user accesses the affected pages, potentially leading to data theft, session hijacking, or further exploitation of the website.
recommended_actions: Update the Yoast SEO plugin to a patched version as soon as it becomes available.
Restrict Contributor-level access to only trusted users and review user permissions to minimize exposure.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious scripts targeting this vulnerability.
